examples: The movie was entertaining from start to finish.

definitions.text: The beginning point of a race, a board game, etc.
examples: Captured pieces are returned to the start of the board.

definitions.text: To begin, commence, initiate. To set in motion.
examples: to start a stream of water; to start a rumour; to start a business
The President fired the gun to start the footrace.
to start a stream of water; to start a rumour; to start a business
The President fired the gun to start the footrace.

definitions.text: A projection or protrusion; that which pokes out.
The curved or inclined front and bottom of a water wheel bucket.
The arm, or level, of a gin, drawn around by a horse.
